rationale: Both papers are closely aligned in theoretical channel-power modeling: the cited 2006 JMRK paper develops bilateral bargaining with asymmetric competing retailers (e.g., dominant low-cost retailer effects), while the citing 2010 paper explicitly studies a powerful retailer’s channel-structure choice in a game-theoretic framework. Given this strong conceptual/method overlap and plausible use as a key prior mechanism/comparison source, this is not an obvious incidental mention.
